title: Bridge Essay: Cognitive styles diversity and group creativity: Evidence from fNIRS hyperscanni

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ritual** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.

**Cognitive styles diversity and group creativity: Evidence from fNIRS hyperscanning.** — Behavioural brain research (2026). PMID: . DOI: 10.1016/j.bbr.2025.115986. Score: 27. This study explores how cognitive style diversity (differences in information processing among team members) influences group creativity through interpersonal neural mechanisms. 116 college students were assigned to high-diversity (HD) groups (mix of field-independent and field-dependent individuals) or low-diversity (LD) groups (homogeneous cognitive styles).
